message:
fix(server): correct shutdown test phase wait targets for race safety

Fix two shutdown tests that had incorrect wait_for_phase targets:

- test force_close_sets_force_closing_phase: This test spawns a task
  that calls force_close() then mark_stopped(). The assertion checks
  ForceClosing. Previously waited for Stopped which could race past
  ForceClosing. Now waits for ForceClosing to observe the intermediate
  phase reliably.

- test trigger_immediate_advances_to_stopped: This test spawns a task
  that calls trigger_immediate() which goes straight to Stopped. The
  assertion checks Stopped. Previously waited for ForceClosing which
  is never reached by trigger_immediate(). Now waits for Stopped to
  match the actual signal path.

Note: This corrects commit eea2042c which swapped both tests to the
same target, when in fact they need different targets because they test
different shutdown paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
